Job.title: Computer Numerical Control (CNC) Machinist
Job.description: Aureol Global Connections is seeking experienced CNC Machinists on behalf of Gibney Steel Products Limited located at Moylagh, Oldcastle, Co. Meath, A82 KT97 to join their skilled production team. The successful candidate will be responsible for setting up and operating CNC machinery, including press brake machines, while interpreting production drawings to manufacture components to specification. The role also involves carrying out quality checks, meeting daily production targets, maintaining equipment and work areas, and strictly following all health and safety procedures. Candidates may also be required to work within our robotic welding cell (training provided) and carry out ad-hoc production duties as needed. The ideal candidate will have good attention to detail, the ability to follow instructions accurately, good teamwork and communication skills, and a reliable, safety-conscious attitude. Previous CNC or manufacturing experience is an advantage, but training will be provided for the suitable candidate. This is a full-time role of 48 hours per week with an annual salary range of €45,052.80-€48,000. Interested applicants should send their CV to info@aureolglobalconnections.com.

Job.title: Data Governance Analyst
Job.description: About CodeWave CodeWave is a practical IT consultancy focused on data, cloud, and digital transformation. We help clients design governance models that improve data trust, compliance, and decision quality across business and technical teams. Our delivery style blends structured governance with hands-on execution. About The Role As a Data Governance Analyst, you will support governance operations across client programs, including data quality controls, GDPR-aligned access processes, and reporting workflows. You will work closely with consultants, analysts, and stakeholders to keep data accurate, well-documented, and audit-ready. This role is ideal for professionals with 2-3 years of governance, data quality, or analytics support experience who want to deepen their governance and compliance expertise. Key Responsibilities Support data governance operations, including data access controls and stewardship workflows. Maintain data quality checks, validation routines, and issue tracking logs. Prepare governance documentation, including policies, glossaries, and data dictionaries. Assist with GDPR-aligned processes for access, retention, and incident response. Coordinate reporting in Excel or Power BI and ensure metrics are accurate and current. Support training and onboarding for governance tools and data processes. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support digital transformation initiatives. Required Skills & Experience 2-3 years in data governance, data quality, analytics support, or related roles. Working knowledge of GDPR and data handling best practices. Experience with Excel and reporting; Power BI exposure is a strong advantage. Strong documentation skills and attention to detail in data processes. Clear communication and stakeholder coordination skills. What We Offer Mentorship from senior governance and data specialists. Exposure to enterprise data programs and regulated industries. Structured learning support for governance and analytics tools. Visa sponsorship for non-EU applicants, subject to eligibility and role requirements. Initial accommodation support can be arranged to support relocation.

Job.title: HGV/ARTIC Drivers
Job.description:
ROSKELL TRANSPORT LIMITED requires qualified HGV/ARTIC DRIVERS. The position offers a minimum package of €36,605. Approx 39 hours per week, 5 days over 7. Description: Duties include ensuring the collection and delivery of customer goods in a professional and safe manner. The location of work is starting in Dublin and travelling from A to B throughout Ireland delivering loads. Candidates must hold relevant licence/CPCs and accident free driving history. For more information contact: transport@roskell.ie

**Contact us now with your CV and to hear about the other benefits on offer**
**Accommodation assistance may be provided to applicants relocating to Dublin**

Job.title: Geotechnical Engineer
Job.description: Our client, Gutyiera Limited, based in Donabate, Co. Dublin, is currently seeking a Geotechnical Engineer for immediate start at their facility in Turvey Lane, Donabate, Co. Dublin, K36 P589. Qualifications & Requirements: - Bachelor’s Degree in Geotechnical Engineering, Civil Engineering, or a related field - Proven experience in soil stabilisation techniques and applications - Strong knowledge of ground improvement methods and materials - Ability to analyse geotechnical data and prepare detailed reports - Proficiency in geotechnical software and tools - Excellent communication and project management skills - Minimum 2 years of experience in a similar role General Duties / Responsibilities / Experience: - To apply geological knowledge for the assessment of sites, soils, slope stability, sediments and other observable characteristics in the earth - To integrate the relevant information into the planning and development of projects in those area - To assess and answer questions regarding the geological characteristics of soils by performing research and experiments of the selected sites - To conduct and lead soil stabilisation projects in the construction of roads, platforms and residential sites - To conduct geotechnical investigations and assessments - To design and implement soil stabilisation solutions (lime/cement treatment, geosynthetics) - To analyse and interpret soil data, providing recommendations for ground improvement - To ensure compliance with environmental and safety regulations. - To liaise with clients, contractors, and suppliers to ensure project objectives are met - Essential knowledge includes civil engineering, engineering principles and engineering processes - Further essential knowledge includes environmental legislation, geographic information systems and technical drawings - Applicants must have knowledge of geological time scales, geology, mining, and construction & civil engineering machinery - Essential skills include adjustment of engineering designs, advising on building matters, and approving engineering designs - Further essential skills include assessment of environmental impact, compliance with safety legislation, and execution of feasibility studies - Candidates must be competent to perform sample testing, perform scientific research on sites, and use technical drawing software - Candidates must have experience in projects involving soil stabilisation, ground improvement and innovative geotechnical solutions - Experience in soil stabilisation in the construction of roads, platforms and residential sites is required - Minimum 2 years of experience in a similar role is required Annual Remuneration: €45,000.00; Pay rate €22.19 per hour Work Location: Gutyiera Limited, Unit 12 Shannon Valley Centre, Turvey Lane, Donabate, Co. Dublin, K36 P589 Number of hours work each week: 39 If you are interested in this role, please apply with an up-to-date CV to info@crewitresourcing.com or at the link below.
